behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: This behavior tests whether the system correctly identifies explicit images. The failure isn't about flagging logic—it's about whether the real vision model accurately classifies an image as explicit. A mock that always returns 'explicit' would pass while real explicit images go undetected. The success depends fundamentally on what the real AI vision model produces; no amount of integration testing with mocked responses can validate this real-world classification accuracy.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a real image known to contain explicit content to the system. Verify the vision model classifies it correctly and the system flags and holds the image without publishing. Run multiple times with different explicit images to validate the model's reliability across variations.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: This specifically tests the text model's ability to handle adversarial inputs (obfuscation). A mock that always detects hate speech passes trivially and doesn't validate the model's robustness to obfuscation. The real failure is: the model fails on obfuscated hate speech in production. This depends entirely on what the real text model can recognize. Integration tests with mocked detection cannot reveal whether the real model handles these edge cases.
behaviors.test_description: Create test cases with hate speech using various obfuscation techniques: letter substitution (e.g., 'h@te'), unicode lookalikes (е instead of e), number substitution (4 for a), spacing variations. Submit each to the system via the real text model. Verify the model flags all variations consistently.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: This tests the system's decision logic: IF (image_clean AND text_clean) THEN publish ELSE hold. The failure is structural—the system incorrectly drops clean posts or holds them despite both checks passing. This can be caught with mocked AI models (both returning 'clean') because we're validating the conditional logic, not the accuracy of the models themselves. The logic doesn't depend on what the real models actually produce.
behaviors.test_description: Mock both the vision and text models to return 'clean' classifications. Submit a test post and verify it transitions to published status immediately. Verify it does NOT enter the hold/review queue. Test with multiple post variations (text-only, image-only, mixed) to ensure the logic applies across all post types.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: Latency is a runtime infrastructure property that only appears under real conditions. Mocking the AI models with instant responses masks the real bottleneck: network round-trips to external vision and text APIs. A test with mocked zero-latency responses will pass even if real API calls would timeout. The failure (pipeline takes >5 seconds) only surfaces when calling actual external services. This requires System-level testing with real external API calls.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a post with both an image and text to the system. Measure end-to-end latency from submission to completion. Repeat 10+ times during different times of day (simulating varying API response times). Verify all runs complete within 5 seconds. Monitor API response times and processing time separately to identify bottlenecks. Test with concurrent submissions to identify queue-related delays.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: This tests error-handling logic: IF (vision_unavailable) THEN queue_for_retry ELSE process. A mocked vision model that raises an exception can verify the system doesn't auto-approve on error. The logic failure (auto-approving despite unavailability) is caught by Integration-level testing. While System-level testing with a real service down would be more realistic, the core failure—incorrect handling of the error condition—is detectable with Integration tests. The minimum viable test mocks the unavailability and checks the system's response.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the vision model to raise a service unavailability exception (e.g., timeout, 503 error). Submit an image post. Verify the post is added to a retry queue, not auto-approved or silently dropped. Verify the system logs the failure. Test multiple failure scenarios (timeout, connection error, malformed response) to ensure the error handling is robust.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: Consistency requires either (1) system-level caching of decisions, or (2) the AI model being deterministic. If the system caches, this is testable at Integration level: mock the vision model, verify it's called once, then verify the cached result is returned on subsequent submissions. The failure is either a cache miss or premature expiration, both detectable via mocking. However, if the system doesn't cache, the test requires the real model (Agentic). The most likely scenario in a high-throughput system is caching, making Integration the minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a borderline image (one that is ambiguous between safe/explicit) to the system. Record the decision and timestamp. Submit the exact same image again within 30 seconds. Verify the decision is identical. Submit again after cache expiration (e.g., 5 minutes) and verify a new decision is made. Test with different borderline images to ensure consistency isn't accidental. If possible, inspect system logs to confirm the image was cached and not re-evaluated.
